metric_name: Weekly PC market trend commentary
metric_value: Nothing very exciting happening in desktop deals this week. Dell has replaced its Dimension 2350 with the newer 2400, but we have yet to notice any difference in these models other than a price drop.
notes: Kastner market assessment for week of 2003-07-13

metric_name: BestBuy: buying tips for week of 2003-07-13
metric_value: Best Buy has revamped its website completely. You'll find the link to its "weekly ad" at the bottom, center of the home page along with the "store locator" link.

metric_name: Circuit City: buying tips for week of 2003-07-13
metric_value: Save up to $200 on select Desktop and Laptop Computer Packages and enjoy no interest for 12 months. Circuitcity.com continues its free shipping offer on all its online items.

metric_name: CompUSA: buying tips for week of 2003-07-13
metric_value: CompUSA.com tends to continue selling "older" PC models after BestBuy.com and CircuitCity.com have removed them. This means you can get better deals on models that retailers want off the shelves to make room for the newer versions.

metric_name: Dell: buying tips for week of 2003-07-13
metric_value: Dell currently offers free shipping and CD-RW upgrades in the second optical bay on all desktop models. With the exception of the 2400, Dell offers mail-in rebates this week: $50 off the 4600 and 4600C and $150 off the 8300 and XPS models.

metric_name: eMachines: buying tips for week of 2003-07-13
metric_value: Circuit City and Best Buy sell all four models in eMachines' new line-up and they continue to sell a few older models. Bundled deals from these national retailers supply the best bang for your buck on eMachines desktops.

metric_name: Gateway: buying tips for week of 2003-07-13
metric_value: Gateway's prices on the PCs we customize at www.gateway.com have not changed, with the exception of a slight price drop on the 500X model last week. But we were happy to see that the 700XL, while maintaining the same price, now comes standard with the new Pentium 4 HT 3.2 GHz ...

metric_name: Sony: buying tips for week of 2003-07-13
metric_value: Sony.com offers a plethora of models right now, although, like eMachines, the best deals usually come from bundled retail offers. Receive free shipping at Sony Online this week.

metric_name: HP and Compaq: buying tips for week of 2003-07-13
metric_value: HP Online offers competitive prices on its retail models, although national retailers usually carry well-priced bundled deals. HP has expanded its A250 line to resemble the S4000 series of desktops by Compaq so that choosing the grade of processor determines the model you will...

metric_name: Intel Pentium 4 HT: positioning in week of 2003-07-13
metric_value: Customize a high-end PC with the latest Hyper-Threading Intel processor with 3.2 GHz continues to yield high prices, especially in contrast with HP's winning machine in our $1600+ category so we have not included any Dell PCs with this technology yet.

metric_value: Customize a high-end PC with the latest Hyper-Threading Intel processor with 3.2 GHz continues to yield high prices, especially in contrast with HP's winning machine in our $1600+ category so we have not included any Dell PCs with this technology yet.

metric_name: AMD Athlon: positioning in week of 2003-07-13
metric_value: The A220n and A230n contain AMD Athlon processors at very attractive prices.

metric_name: Windows Media Center PC: positioning in week of 2003-07-13
metric_value: And we're expecting more HP Media Center PCs to appear in these flyers in the coming weeks.
